title: Loving-Kindness (Metta)
blurb: Cultivate warmth toward yourself and others with simple phrases.
lead: Loving-kindness, or metta, is the practice of deliberately cultivating goodwill — toward yourself, loved ones, and eventually all beings.

blocks.text: Metta meditation uses gentle repeated phrases to cultivate warmth and goodwill — beginning with yourself and widening outward.
Research links loving-kindness practice to increased positive emotion, self-compassion, and connection, and reduced self-criticism.

items: Sit comfortably and take a few settling breaths.
Bring yourself to mind and silently repeat: 'May I be safe. May I be well. May I be at ease.'
Let the words be an intention, not a demand — it's okay if the feeling is faint.
Bring to mind someone you love and offer them the same phrases.
Gradually widen to a neutral person, a difficult person, and finally all beings.

blocks.text: Expecting to instantly feel love. The practice plants a seed; the feeling grows with repetition, not force.

resources.title: Self-compassion & loving-kindness practices
